fixed_conditions: same tip; nearby locations; same setpoint; same velocity
primary_readouts: extend minimum; retract pull-off; rupture distance; hysteresis work
decision_rule: water-bridge mechanism is strongly supported if giant retract adhesion collapses in dry gas and changes systematically with RH

levels: approach/retract velocity over at least four levels spanning about one decade around the current setting
fixed_conditions: fixed RH; same tip; same sample region
primary_readouts: pull-off force; rupture distance; dissipated work; rupture probability
decision_rule: velocity dependence or a critical speed links the effect to water bridge nucleation/growth and squeeze-out dynamics

primary_readouts: pull-off versus dwell time; rupture distance; hysteresis work
decision_rule: growth or saturation with dwell time indicates bridge growth or contact-line aging

levels: suspended COF; supported COF on SiN/Si; blank SiN/Si; copper-grid sample
fixed_conditions: same RH; tip; velocity; force setpoint
primary_readouts: pull-off/extend ratio; hysteresis work; sample ranking
decision_rule: selective amplification in suspended COF supports compliant-membrane coupling rather than ordinary rigid-substrate capillarity

primary_readouts: force scaling with nominal tip radius; rupture distance
decision_rule: classic capillarity should scale roughly with tip radius; deviations imply membrane deformation, pinning, or multi-contact geometry

levels: native tip; hydrophobized tip; cleaned/hydrophilic control; optional COF chemistry variants
decision_rule: chemistry dependence establishes the role of water affinity and contact-line pinning

levels: KPFM; grounded sample; optional bias sweep
primary_readouts: long-range force offset; snap-in distance; adhesion force
decision_rule: if bias/charge explains the attraction, water-bridge claims must be weakened or separated
